name: Cardiovascular System — Blood & Heart
description: 40 illustrated MCQs on blood, cardiac anatomy, the conduction system and coronary circulation.
author: MJ Technology Solutions

questions.question: Which important feature is TRUE of a mature red blood cell?
options: It has a large central nucleus
It is produced in the liver
It has no nucleus and is flexible
questions.explanation: Mature RBCs have no nucleus, are small and flexible, and live about 120 days.

questions.question: The oxygen-carrying molecule that gives red blood cells their colour is:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin binds oxygen and gives RBCs their red colour.
questions.imageAlt: Oxygen binding to haemoglobin in red blood cells

questions.question: The normal lifespan of a red blood cell is approximately:
questions.explanation: Red blood cells live about 120 days before being destroyed in the liver and spleen.

questions.question: A reduction in red blood cell count leads to the condition known as:
questions.explanation: A reduced RBC count results in anaemia.

questions.question: The main function of leucocytes (white blood cells) is to:
options: Protect against bacteria, viruses, parasites and toxins
questions.explanation: White blood cells protect the body from harmful bacteria, viruses, parasites and toxic substances.
questions.imageAlt: The five main types of white blood cell

questions.question: Platelets (thrombocytes) are best described as:
questions.explanation: Platelets are cell fragments made in red bone marrow that promote blood clotting.

questions.question: Approximately what percentage of plasma is water?
questions.explanation: Plasma is a clear liquid that is about 92% water.

questions.question: Which plasma proteins increase osmotic pressure and include clotting factors and antibodies?
questions.explanation: Plasma proteins such as fibrinogen and antibodies raise osmotic pressure.

questions.question: On average, about how many times per minute does the heart beat?
questions.explanation: The heart pumps about 70 times per minute.

questions.question: The heart is located in the thorax:
options: Entirely on the right of the midline
On the superior surface of the diaphragm, left of the midline
questions.explanation: The heart sits on the superior surface of the diaphragm, left of the midline, posterior to the sternum.
questions.imageAlt: Location of the heart in the thoracic cavity

questions.question: The double-walled sac that surrounds the heart is the:
questions.explanation: The pericardium is a double-walled sac surrounding the heart and roots of the great vessels.

questions.question: The visceral layer of the serous pericardium is also known as the:
questions.explanation: The visceral layer of the serous pericardium is the epicardium, lining the heart surface.

questions.question: The muscular layer forming the bulk of the heart wall is the:
questions.explanation: The myocardium is the cardiac muscle layer forming the bulk of the heart wall.

questions.question: Which is NOT a function of the pericardium?
questions.explanation: The pericardium protects, anchors and prevents overfilling; the heartbeat is generated by the SA node.

questions.question: The receiving chambers of the heart are the:
questions.explanation: The atria are the receiving chambers; the ventricles are the discharging chambers.
questions.imageAlt: Internal chambers and valves of the heart

questions.question: Blood enters the left atrium from the:
questions.explanation: The left atrium receives oxygenated blood from the pulmonary veins.

questions.question: Muscular ridges called pectinate muscles mark the walls of the:
questions.explanation: Pectinate muscles mark the atrial walls.
questions.imageAlt: Atria and pectinate muscles of the heart

questions.question: Papillary muscles and trabeculae carneae are found in the:
questions.explanation: Papillary muscles and trabeculae carneae (muscular ridges) mark the ventricular walls.
questions.imageAlt: Ventricles with papillary muscles and trabeculae carneae

questions.question: Why is the wall of the left ventricle the thickest chamber wall?
options: It must pump blood into the systemic circulation
It receives blood from the lungs
questions.explanation: The left ventricle wall is thickest because it pumps blood into the high-resistance systemic circulation.

questions.question: The valve located between the right atrium and right ventricle is the:
questions.explanation: The tricuspid (right AV) valve lies between the right atrium and right ventricle.

questions.question: The atrioventricular valves are anchored to the papillary muscles by the:
questions.explanation: Chordae tendineae anchor the AV valves to the papillary muscles, preventing backflow.

questions.question: The aortic semilunar valve lies between the:
questions.explanation: The aortic semilunar valve lies between the left ventricle and the aorta.

questions.question: In the pathway of blood through the heart, blood leaving the right ventricle passes through the pulmonary semilunar valve into the:
questions.explanation: The right ventricle pumps blood through the pulmonary semilunar valve into the pulmonary arteries toward the lungs.

questions.question: Cardiac muscle cells are joined end-to-end by structures that anchor cells and allow ion passage, called:
questions.explanation: Intercalated discs interconnect cardiac cells and allow free passage of ions.
questions.imageAlt: Intercalated disc with gap junctions and desmosomes

questions.question: Within intercalated discs, which junctions propagate action potentials between cardiac cells?
questions.explanation: Gap junctions link cardiac cells electrically to propagate action potentials; desmosomes convey contractile force.

questions.question: Because cardiac muscle acts as a coordinated unit, it is described as a functional:
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle behaves as a functional syncytium — cells contract as a single unit.

questions.question: The natural pacemaker of the heart is the:
questions.explanation: The SA node creates the excitation signal (action potential) that initiates each heartbeat.
questions.imageAlt: The conduction system of the heart

questions.question: In the conduction system, the electrical signal is briefly DELAYED at the:
questions.explanation: The signal is delayed at the AV node, allowing the atria to finish contracting before the ventricles.

questions.question: Which structure spreads the impulse rapidly through the ventricular walls, causing them to contract?
questions.explanation: The bundle of His and Purkinje fibres spread the impulse through the ventricles, causing contraction.

questions.question: The two coronary arteries that supply the heart arise from the:
options: Aortic sinuses (first branches of the aorta)
questions.explanation: The right and left coronary arteries arise from the aortic sinuses — the first branches of the aorta.

questions.question: The left anterior descending artery mainly supplies the:
options: Anterior 2/3 of the interventricular septum and adjoining left ventricle
questions.explanation: The LAD supplies the anterior two-thirds of the interventricular septum and adjoining left ventricular wall.

questions.question: Most of the venous blood from the heart muscle drains into the right atrium via the:
questions.explanation: Cardiac veins join to form the coronary sinus, which opens into the right atrium.

questions.question: The largest vein of the heart, about 3 cm long and lying in the posterior coronary sulcus, is the:
questions.explanation: The coronary sinus is the largest cardiac vein (~3 cm) in the posterior coronary sulcus.

questions.question: Sympathetic nerve stimulation of the heart tends to:
options: Increase heart rate and dilate coronary arteries
questions.explanation: Sympathetic (cardio-acceleratory) nerves increase heart rate and dilate coronary arteries.

questions.question: Parasympathetic supply to the heart is carried mainly by which nerve?
questions.explanation: Parasympathetic (cardio-inhibitory) fibres run via the vagus nerve, slowing the heart rate.

questions.question: The hepatic portal circulation carries blood from the:
questions.explanation: The hepatic portal system carries partially deoxygenated, nutrient-rich blood from the gut to the liver.

questions.question: Angina pectoris and acute myocardial infarction are features of which condition?
questions.explanation: Angina pectoris and myocardial infarction are manifestations of ischaemic heart disease.

questions.question: A congenital narrowing of the aorta is termed:
questions.explanation: Coarctation of the aorta is a congenital narrowing of the aorta.

questions.question: Congestive heart failure may result from all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Congestive heart failure follows reduced contractility, diminished coronary flow or damaged valves.

questions.question: An atrial septal defect is classified as a:
questions.explanation: Atrial septal defect is a congenital abnormality of the heart.
